_description: Codex CLI version support matrix for the MeowKit codex bundle. min-candidate is the tentative supported floor; latest-verified is the current stable release the bundle is validated against. Confirmed against the packaged-install compatibility lane before the operational floor (CODEX_MIN_SUPPORTED_VERSION) is lowered.
minCandidateBasis: Hooks became stable (no [features] flag, inline config.toml) at Codex 0.124.0 per the openai/codex changelog announcement.
operationalFloorNote: CODEX_MIN_SUPPORTED_VERSION in providers/codex/capabilities.ts. Kept above the min-candidate until the packaged-install compatibility lane confirms the bundle's actually-used surfaces work at 0.124.0; the floor is lowered only after that confirmation.

features.note: Stable, inline config.toml support since 0.124.0. Hooks enabled by default.
Legacy [profiles.x] TOML sections deprecated since 0.134.0 — standalone ~/.codex/<name>.config.toml is the current mechanism. Bundle installs NO [profiles.x] sections.
--strict-config is Stable; used by the compatibility lane to reject config fields a version does not recognize.
Experimental. Capability/version-gated install with fallback; never sole enforcement.

Only high and xhigh are officially documented (xhigh is codex-max-tier-only, for non-latency-sensitive tasks). Full enum (none/minimal/low/medium/high/xhigh) is NOT officially enumerated on a single page. CLI does not validate the value: `codex exec --strict-config -c model_reasoning_effort=bogus` accepted 'bogus' and did not error (override layer bypasses strict-config validation). Phase-5 agent effort values are therefore gated to the documented set: use `high` as the deep-class ceiling; `xhigh` only if a shipped default model is confirmed codex-max-tier.

displacementSpike._note: Test-displacement spike measured 2026-07-23 (Node 24, `vitest run codex`, 127-test baseline all green). Records how many tests FAIL when bundle manifest entries are flipped active, so the cutover flip sequence plans against measured numbers instead of the 41-test estimate.

tests: codex-authored-bundle.test.ts > the migrate overlay is inert while every entry is draft (active:false)
hooksJsonActiveOnly.note: Only the intentional inert-guard failed. ZERO of the converter-shaped migrate tests (acceptance/e2e/characterization/agents-md-budget) failed at the hooks.json flip.

tests: codex-authored-bundle.test.ts > the migrate overlay is inert while every entry is draft (active:false)
migrate-codex-e2e.test.ts > leaves zero toolkit branding and no toolkit files in the target project
allEntriesActive.note: Upper bound with every surface flipped. The e2e branding failure means the CURRENT authored AGENTS.md/agents/skills content still carries toolkit-brand strings the brand-free gate rejects — a real cleanup item for the native re-authoring in phases 4-6.

displacementSpike.interpretation: Measured displacement is far below the 41-test estimate BECAUSE today's authored bundle is still porter-generated and therefore byte-similar to converter output, so most converter-shaped assertions still pass on flip. The 41 is the POPULATION of converter-shaped test blocks at risk; actual failures grow as phases 4-6 hand-author content that diverges from converter output. Phase-9 flip staging should re-measure per surface after each surface is re-authored, not rely on this floor. The inert-guard test itself must be rewritten (or its assertion inverted) as the first surface flips active.
